title: A School Stabbing: What Happened?
summary: A teenage girl was stabbed at a high school called Thorpe St Andrew School near Norwich. She got minor injuries and was taken to the hospital. Police came quickly and arrested a 16-year-old boy for the stabbing. The school was put on lockdown, which means students had to stay in their classrooms for safety. The road near the school, Laundry Lane, was closed. Police say this stabbing is not connected to another problem at the school last week. Parents are worried because they haven't gotten much information about what happened. The school trust said they can't talk more right now. This is a scary event that shows why safety at school is important.

keywords.explanation: hurt with a sharp object like a knife
when people stay inside a building for safety
taken by police because they might have done something wrong
small hurts that are not very serious
moms and dads of the students

options: They want to go to school

background_read: This article is about a stabbing at a high school in England. Stabbings are when someone uses a knife to hurt another person, and they can be very dangerous. Schools sometimes go into lockdown during emergencies to protect students by keeping them inside classrooms. Police help by investigating crimes and arresting suspects. Parents often feel scared when bad things happen at school because they want their children to be safe. It's important for schools and police to share information so everyone knows what's going on.
Article_Structure: WHO: A teenage girl, a 16-year-old boy, police, parents, and students at Thorpe St Andrew School.
WHAT: A stabbing incident where a girl was hurt, and a boy was arrested; the school was put on lockdown.
WHERE: At Thorpe St Andrew School near Norwich, on Laundry Lane.
WHY: To report on a safety incident at a school and how authorities responded to keep people safe.

perspectives.description: The incident highlights worries about violence in schools and the need for better protection.
